NVDA.title: Verkada takes Nvidia investment to expand its physical AI platform
NVDA.summary: Verkada Inc., a physical security company, has secured an investment from Nvidia Corp. and formed a technical partnership to enhance the AI capabilities across its 2.4 million connected devices. This collaboration will utilize Nvidia's Cosmos family of world foundation models and the Physical AI Data Factory toolkit to improve video search accuracy by enabling the identification of specific individuals, objects, or moments across recordings. The deal aims to accelerate Verkada's AI development for applications such as school safety, worker protection, retail theft prevention, and operational efficiency, building on its recent funding round that valued the company at $5.8 billion.

NVDA.title: This Hidden Aerospace Stock Is Up 470% in the Last 5 Years and Just Hit a New All-Time High
NVDA.summary: GE Aerospace, one of the three entities from the former General Electric conglomerate, has seen its stock price soar by 473.4% over the last five years, reaching a new all-time high. This growth is attributed to its strong market position as a duopoly in jet engine manufacturing, its lucrative aftermarket services business which underpins a $181 billion backlog, and robust free cash flow generation. The company, led by CEO Larry Culp, prioritizes shareholder returns through buybacks and dividends, indicating potential for continued bullish performance.

NVDA.title: Why Barrett (BBSI) Stock Is Trading Up Today
NVDA.summary: Shares of Barrett Business Services (BBSI) jumped 5.8% today due to a broad rally in the staffing and employment services sector and investor focus on the company's capital return program, specifically its share buybacks. The company repurchased 700,926 shares for $20.1 million in Q1 2026 and shareholders recently approved an expanded stock incentive plan. Despite a prior drop four months ago due to missing Q4 financial expectations, BBSI stock is up 2.9% year-to-date.

NVDA.title: Corning Inc Stock (GLW) Moved Down by 14.06% on Jul 1: What Signal Does It Send?
NVDA.summary: Corning Inc (GLW) stock dropped 14.06% on July 1st due to aggressive profit-taking, concerns over its high price-to-earnings ratio, and significant insider selling. The decline was also fueled by bearish options activity and fundamental concerns, including missed revenue guidance and delays in solar wafer production. Although long-term optimism exists due to AI-related optical fiber partnerships, the market is repricing the stock to reflect a more conservative short-term risk profile.

NVDA.title: Western Digital Corp Stock (WDC) Moved Down by 7.03% on Jul 1: What Investors Need To Know
NVDA.summary: Western Digital Corp (WDC) shares dropped by 7.03% due to widespread profit-taking in the technology sector and broader macroeconomic pressures, despite Bank of America raising its price target to $732. The decline is attributed to systemic sector rotation, hawkish Federal Reserve signals, an antitrust lawsuit, and concerns over future storage demand. Additionally, technical factors like debt retirement, SanDisk share swap arbitrage, and extensive insider selling contributed to the downward momentum.

NVDA.title: Nvidia, Tesla, Caterpillar: ETFs Most Exposed to Michael Burry's New Shorts
NVDA.summary: Michael Burry, known for predicting the 2008 housing collapse, has placed bearish bets against Nvidia, Caterpillar, Tesla, and Applied Materials, indicating concerns about their valuations amid the AI investment cycle. These companies are major holdings in popular ETFs, including semiconductor and technology funds, as well as industrial ETFs that benefit from AI infrastructure development. If Burry's skepticism proves correct, ETF investors heavily exposed to these stocks could face significant impacts.

NVDA.title: Why KLA Corporation (KLAC) Stock Is Down Today
NVDA.summary: KLA Corporation (KLAC) shares dropped 12.3% in a broad semiconductor sell-off, as investors took profits after a record-breaking first half for the sector. This downturn follows previous volatility triggered by reports of SK Hynix slowing its HBM expansion, which initially sparked fears about the AI chip market but was identified as a margin story. The current dip appears to be profit-taking after significant gains, with some analysts viewing it as a buying opportunity.

NVDA.title: SanDisk Corporation Stock (SNDK) Moved Down by 9.91% on Jul 1: What Investors Need To Know
NVDA.summary: SanDisk Corporation (SNDK) experienced a 9.91% stock decline on July 1st, primarily due to profit-taking by investors shifting to a risk-off stance amidst broader market softening. This downturn occurred despite positive analyst updates, including an upward revision of its price target from Bank of America, highlighting strong NAND flash demand. Factors contributing to the sell-off included SanDisk's premium valuation, recent elevated insider selling, and its significant exposure to tech-concentrated ETFs, making it vulnerable to amplified sell orders during market rotations.

MSFT.title: Should You Buy Wix.com Ltd. (WIX)’s Shares?
MSFT.summary: Wix.com Ltd. (NASDAQ:WIX) has seen a significant drop in its share price, down 71% over the past year and 55% year-to-date, despite efforts to integrate AI into its platforms. The company recently partnered with OpenAI for its Codex Enterprise platform and Microsoft for its 365 Copilot, aiming to enhance user experience within these ecosystems. While acknowledging the risks, the article suggests that other AI stocks might offer greater return potential.

MSFT.title: Chevron Corporation (CVX) Partners with Microsoft to Power Texas Data Center
MSFT.summary: Chevron Corporation has partnered with Microsoft to develop Project Kilby, a co-located natural gas-fired power facility in Texas. This facility will provide dedicated electricity to Microsoft's data center campus for 20 years, expanding its capacity by 2 gigawatts and supporting thousands of jobs. Chevron is expected to make a final investment decision on the project by year-end, collaborating with Engine No 1 and GE Vernova.

MSFT.title: CrowdStrike Holdings, Inc. Class A Trade Ideas — SIX:CRWD.USD
MSFT.summary: This article compiles various trade ideas and analyses for CrowdStrike Holdings, Inc. (CRWD), predominantly expressing bullish sentiment due to its AI integration, strong financial performance, and market leadership in cybersecurity. Multiple contributors provide technical outlooks, price targets, and fundamental justifications, including the acquisition of Pangea, ambitious long-term targets, and strategic partnerships, although some express caution regarding valuation and potential deceleration in growth. The overarching theme is CRWD's strong position in the growing cybersecurity market, driven by AI and cloud-native solutions.

MSFT.title: Engine No. 1-backed Joulent gets $1.75 billion minority investment
MSFT.summary: Joulent, backed by Engine No. 1, has secured a $1.75 billion minority investment from National Grid. This investment formalizes Joulent's ownership in a significant project with Chevron and Microsoft, which involves building a 2.67-GW power plant to supply a Microsoft data center near the Permian Basin. The deal highlights Joulent's expansion and its strategic partnerships in the energy sector.

MSFT.title: Docusign showcases AI assistant and agents at Momentum London 2026 to power the next era of agreement work
MSFT.summary: Docusign unveiled new AI-powered capabilities, including an AI assistant and agents, at its Momentum London 2026 conference. These innovations, built on Docusign's Intelligent Agreement Management (IAM) platform and powered by its Iris AI engine, aim to accelerate agreement reviews, simplify web form creation, and integrate seamlessly with other business systems through an open platform. The company highlighted how these advancements help businesses like Experian, Perceptyx, and Aon reduce cycle times and manual effort in contract management.

MSFT.title: International Business Machines Corp Stock (IBM) Moved Up by 3.90% on Jul 1: What Investors Need To Know
MSFT.summary: International Business Machines Corp (IBM) stock rose 3.90% on July 1st, driven by technological breakthroughs in semiconductor engineering and quantum computing, and favorable analyst upgrades due to its hybrid cloud growth. Despite some long-term commercialization timelines and valuation concerns, the company’s strategic partnerships and regulatory support for its AI and quantum initiatives are maintaining a bullish outlook. Institutional confidence has been bolstered by these factors, although intraday volatility persists due to execution and market risks as well as company-specific challenges like enterprise AI adoption friction and a debt burden from acquisitions.

MSFT.title: Microsoft, Amazon seen 'standing out' from strong cloud spending, Jefferies says (MSFT:NASDAQ)
MSFT.summary: Jefferies notes that Microsoft and Amazon are leaders in cloud computing, with cloud spending expected to accelerate to 10.1% in 2026. Microsoft's Azure is increasingly preferred over Amazon's AWS as a primary provider among CIOs. AI adoption is anticipated to benefit all major cloud providers, but Microsoft and AWS are seen as top beneficiaries, with Google Cloud as a "dark horse."

MSFT.title: Voya Financial stock hits all-time high at 93.16 USD
MSFT.summary: Voya Financial (VOYA) stock reached an all-time high of $93.24, surpassing its 52-week high of $93.05. Despite this, InvestingPro analysis suggests the stock remains undervalued with an attractive PEG ratio of 0.53, reflecting strong performance and investor confidence. The company also reported strong Q1 2026 earnings, exceeding analyst expectations, and received an upgrade to "Strong Buy" from Raymond James due to potential M&A activity.

GOOGL.title: Form 4 Alphabet Inc Class A For: 1 July By Investing.com
GOOGL.summary: This article from Investing.com reports on a Form 4 filing for Alphabet Inc Class A (GOOGL) on July 1st. It also presents a snapshot of various market indices, commodities, and stock prices as of the publishing date, alongside current investment challenges and trending news.

GOOGL.title: Buffett Successor Bet Big on Alphabet, Delta and Macy’s: Are the Moves Paying Off?
GOOGL.summary: Greg Abel, who took over from Warren Buffett as CEO of Berkshire Hathaway at the end of 2025, made several significant changes to the company's investment portfolio in his first quarter, including new purchases of Delta Air Lines, Macy’s, and Alphabet Class C shares. These new positions have collectively generated a profit of approximately $1.34 billion, representing a 36% gain since March 31, 2026. Investors are now watching to see if Abel continues to hold these positions or takes short-term profits.

GOOGL.title: UBS (AMUB) launches GOOGL‑linked auto‑callable securities with 10.05% coupon
GOOGL.summary: UBS AG has launched new Contingent Income Auto-Callable Securities linked to Alphabet Inc. (GOOGL) Class A common stock, offering an annual contingent payment of 10.05% ($25.125 per security) if GOOGL's closing price is at or above 60% of its initial price on specified determination dates. These securities, expected to price on July 10, 2026, and mature around July 13, 2029, can be redeemed early if GOOGL's price meets the call threshold, but investors face capital loss if the final price falls below the downside threshold and do not participate in stock appreciation. The offering highlights various risks, including credit risk of UBS, potential for loss of principal, and limited secondary market liquidity.

GOOGL.title: Alphabet Stock Price Prediction: The Forecast Points to 20% Upside
GOOGL.summary: Alphabet (GOOG) is considered a strong buy by 24/7 Wall St., with a price target of $425.86, indicating a 20.53% upside over the next 12 months from its current trading price of $353.33. The positive outlook is primarily driven by the robust growth of Google Cloud, which saw revenue compound at 63% and has a $462 billion backlog, along with expanding operating margins and significant AI infrastructure investment. While rising CapEx presents a bear case, the article suggests the risk/reward favors owning the stock, with projections showing continued growth to $624 by 2030.

GOOGL.title: Microsoft, Amazon seen 'standing out' from strong cloud spending, Jefferies says (MSFT:NASDAQ)
GOOGL.summary: Jefferies notes that Microsoft and Amazon are leaders in cloud computing, with cloud spending expected to accelerate to 10.1% in 2026. Microsoft's Azure is increasingly preferred over Amazon's AWS as a primary provider among CIOs. AI adoption is anticipated to benefit all major cloud providers, but Microsoft and AWS are seen as top beneficiaries, with Google Cloud as a "dark horse."

GOOGL.title: Final Trades: Unum Group, Alphabet, J.M. Smucker and the IGV
GOOGL.summary: The Investment Committee gives their top stock picks for the second half of the year, focusing on Unum Group, Alphabet, J.M. Smucker, and the IGV. This article highlights the "Final Trades" segment, suggesting these companies and the ETF are important to watch.

GOOGL.title: How Amazon-Backed Data Center Deal And Cost-Sharing Plan At NiSource (NI) Has Changed Its Investment Story
GOOGL.summary: NiSource Inc. received approval for agreements supporting Amazon's new data centers in northern Indiana, including a cost-sharing plan where data center customers will cover infrastructure costs and existing NIPSCO customers will benefit from projected savings and faster regulatory timelines. This deal, along with a prior one for an Alphabet data center, strengthens NiSource's investment narrative through load growth but also increases its exposure to regulatory risks for its multi-year capital plan. While some analysts see significant upside, others are cautious about the company's capital needs and potential impacts of future rate decisions.

GOOGL.title: How Amazon-Backed Data Center Deal And Cost-Sharing Plan At NiSource (NI) Has Changed Its Investment Story
GOOGL.summary: NiSource Inc. recently secured approval for an Amazon-backed data center deal in northern Indiana, which includes new gas-fired generation and battery storage resources, with the data center customers covering infrastructure costs. This framework is expected to generate significant savings for existing NIPSCO customers (around US$1.40 billion) and accelerate regulatory timelines for future data center agreements. The article explores how this development, alongside a previous deal with Alphabet, reinforces NiSource's investment narrative by highlighting load growth and regulatory support, while also noting the risks associated with its extensive capital plan and the need for continued favorable regulatory rulings.
